immutable_core.semantic_definition: A time-stamped respiratory-rate measurement event representing respiratory rate as an event-level ICU vital-sign observation rather than a baseline, summary, score, or stay-level aggregate.
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_respiratory_rate must preserve this event-level respiratory-rate observation meaning.

canonical_representation.range_role_note: This is the current cleaned retained-output range for the governed MIMIC build, not a claim that every raw source row in every database is already valid.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external terminologies as semantic anchors when reliable, but do not make them the backbone of the standard-variable system.
external_alignment.note: A later review may lock an exact or broader vital-sign semantic anchor after the cross-database event boundary is frozen more formally.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing std_respiratory_rate from event-level respiratory rate to first-day or admission baseline respiratory rate
changing std_respiratory_rate from raw measured respiratory rate to ventilator-set or support-specific respiratory-rate constructs
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same event-level respiratory-rate meaning
locking a reviewed external semantic anchor without changing the variable meaning

allowed_database_specific_variation: source code systems and source codes
database-specific timing anchors and local structural keys
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing baseline respiratory rate as std_respiratory_rate
publishing ventilator-set respiratory rate or support-specific variants as std_respiratory_rate

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_respiratory_rate MVP execution should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_respiratory_rate outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
